Planning – Planning Application Documents

Help with this page (opens in a new window)

096654/JO/2011/S1 | Variation of condition no. 3 (specified plans) attached to planning approval 087366/FO/2008/S1 to allow for the inclusion of an air-source heat pump and incorporation of boundary wall into proposed dwelling | 129 Nell Lane Chorlton Manchester M20 2LG Approved
Received
Thu 23 Jun 2011
Validated
Mon 11 Jul 2011
Consultation
Thu 18 Aug 2011
Recommendation and/or Committee
Decided
Mon 19 Sep 2011

Received

  • Application is received by the department either electronically online or via post

Validated

  • The application has been checked for completeness including things such as:
    • The application form has been completed correctly
    • Required supporting documents are present and correct
    • Site Location plan is present
    • Appropriate fee is paid
    • Applicant (or their agent) is contacted to acknowledge the application has been accepted

Consultation

  • We inform relevant parties of your application and invite views. This can include:
    • Putting up site notices
    • Advertising in local media
    • Writing to neighbours
    • Liaising with Statutory consultation bodies

Recommendation and/or Committee

  • The Planning Officer checks the application against local policies and other planning considerations 
  • The Planning Officer checks the views of local residents and statutory bodies who have been liaised with
  • The Planning Officer may undertake a site visit
  • Some applications might not be decided solely by the Planning Officer (called using delegated powers) and some will be referred to Planning & Highways Committee for a decision with an officer's recommendation

Decided

  • We issue a Decision Notice informing the Applicant (or their agent) of the outcome of the application
  • The decision is published online
  • Details

  • Documents (9)
  • Related Cases (2)
